ROUND 1: Graph Strategy: We currently have no Twitter data about the target topic (Bridge scoping test). The highest-priority information gaps are: (1) whether the exact phrase or close variants are being used on Twitter, (2) which tweets/threads contain the phrase and what context/replies they have, and (3) who the influential/organizational accounts are that are posting or being mentioned (to map affiliations). To close these gaps efficiently and coherently, the next round must (A) capture explicit mentions, (B) capture broader variations and top posts, (C) check whether the topic is trending, and (D) drill into the most relevant tweet threads and the author(s) to map affiliations and recent activity. The planned searches use multiple endpoints (search, trends, tweet_thread, timeline, affilates) so results will complement each other and support follow-up network and conversation analysis.

searches_planned.reason: Find exact-phrase, recent posts that explicitly mention 'Bridge scoping test' so we can identify primary tweets and time patterns. Using Latest surfaces the most recent public discussion for immediate relevance.

params.query: bridge scoping OR "scoping test" (bridge OR "bridge inspection" OR "bridge assessment")

searches_planned.reason: Capture broader wording and top/most-engaged tweets about scoping tests related to bridges. This complements the exact-phrase search by surfacing influential posts that may use variant phrasing or industry terms.

Check national trending topics to see if there is any spike in public attention around bridges, inspections, or 'scoping test' related terms. If not trending in the US, we can iterate to other countries. This helps prioritize investigative effort and timing.

Retrieve the full conversation for the top tweet found in the exact-phrase search. This reveals replies, threads, quote-tweets and context (technical details, criticisms, follow-up indicators) necessary to assess the scope and origin of the claim/event.

Fetch the recent posts from the top poster discovered in the searches to understand their activity pattern, whether they are an individual practitioner, journalist, agency, or org, and to find other posts that reference or elaborate on the scoping test.

Map declared affiliations for the same top poster (employer, institution, project links) to assess credibility and identify organizational stakeholders to pursue for technical confirmation or follow-up.
